text: Hello, this is 6 Minute English from BBC Learning English.
zh: 你好,这里是BBC英语教学的《六分钟英语》。

text: I'm Neil.
zh: 我是尼尔。

text: And I'm Bekka.
zh: 我是贝卡。

text: How many of our listeners, Neil, do you think love chocolate?
zh: 尼尔,你觉得我们的听众里有多少人爱吃巧克力?

text: Well, I can't think of anyone who doesn't love chocolate.
zh: 嗯,我想不出有谁不爱吃巧克力。

text: I know I certainly do.
zh: 我自己肯定是爱的。

text: Yes, I thought you might say that, having seen your mid -morning snack most days.
zh: 是啊,看到你几乎每天上午的加餐,我就猜你会这么说。

text: That's true.
zh: 确实。

text: I do love a bit of dark chocolate.
zh: 我确实喜欢来点黑巧克力。

text: Do you know, I really like the one that's got some chilli in it.
zh: 你知道吗,我特别喜欢那种加了辣椒的。

text: What's your favourite, Becca?
zh: 你最喜欢哪种,贝卡?

text: Oh, I also love dark chocolate, but chilli is a bit spicy for me.
zh: 哦,我也爱黑巧克力,不过辣椒对我来说有点辣。

text: I like it with sea salt.
zh: 我喜欢加海盐的。

text: Mmm, that's also good.
zh: 嗯,那种也不错。

text: But now to the stuff that makes chocolate possible.
zh: 但现在说说让巧克力得以存在的东西。

text: Cocoa, a key ingredient, and it's become more expensive in recent years.
zh: 可可,一种关键原料,近年来它变得更贵了。

text: Now, a new EU law could make chocolate even more expensive.
zh: 如今,欧盟一项新法律可能让巧克力变得更贵。

text: Today we'll talk about how this law could change things for farmers, companies and chocolate lovers, as discussed on the recent BBC World Service programme, Business Daily.
zh: 今天我们要聊聊这项法律会如何改变农民、企业和巧克力爱好者的境况,正如最近BBC国际频道节目《商业日报》中所讨论的那样。

text: As always, we'll learn some useful new words and phrases, and you'll find all the vocabulary along with a transcript on our website bbclearningenglish .com.
zh: 和往常一样,我们会学一些实用的新词和短语,你能在我们的网站bbclearningenglish.com上找到全部词汇和文字稿。

text: Before we get started, Neil, I have a question for you.
zh: 在开始之前,尼尔,我有个问题要问你。

text: Europeans are big fans of chocolate, just like us.
zh: 欧洲人非常爱吃巧克力,就像我们一样。

text: Around how many tonnes of cocoa beans were imported to Europe in 2023?
zh: 2023年,大约有多少吨可可豆进口到欧洲?

text: Was it…
a 500 ,000 tonnes, b 2 million tonnes, or c 10 million tonnes?
zh: A,50万吨;B,200万吨;还是C,1000万吨?

text: Well, I have no idea, but I'm just going to guess and say 500 ,000 tonnes.
zh: 嗯,我完全不知道,不过我就猜一个吧,说50万吨。

text: We'll find out if you're right later on.
zh: 我们稍后会揭晓你猜得对不对。

text: Now, that new EU law that we were talking about earlier would stop companies buying cocoa from land where any trees were cut down in the last six years.
zh: 刚才我们提到的那项欧盟新法,会禁止企业从过去六年内有任何树木被砍伐的土地上采购可可。

text: The process of cutting down trees is called deforestation.
zh: 砍伐树木的过程叫作『deforestation』(森林砍伐)。

text: Albert Smith, who owns a small chocolate business, believes that the new law wouldn't change too much for his company.
zh: 拥有一家小型巧克力企业的阿尔伯特·史密斯认为,这项新法不会给他的公司带来太大改变。

text: We have all the information on farm level already because the producers that we work with are small plantations that either work through a co -op or social enterprise.
zh: 我们在农场层面已经掌握了全部信息,因为和我们合作的生产者都是些小种植园,它们要么通过合作社、要么通过社会企业运作。

text: of our cocoa is already traced back to the plantation.
zh: 我们的可可已经能追溯到种植园。

text: Albert adds that they already know where the cocoa beans come from, as they work with farmers from co -ops or social enterprises.
zh: 阿尔伯特补充说,他们已经知道可可豆来自哪里,因为他们与来自合作社或社会企业的农民合作。

text: A co -op in this case is a farm owned by its workers.
zh: 这里的『co-op』指的是一个由其工人所有的农场。

text: A social enterprise is a business that wants to help the community.
zh: 『social enterprise』(社会企业)是一种想帮助社区的企业。

text: Because of working with farmers who are part of these organisations, Albert says that the cocoa they buy can already be traced back to the farms.
zh: 由于与属于这些组织的农民合作,阿尔伯特说,他们采购的可可已经能追溯到农场。

text: farms.
zh: 农场。

text: To trace something back to something means to find out where something has come from.
zh: 『trace something back to something』的意思是查明某物来自哪里。

text: Here's Albert Smith again.
zh: 下面再听阿尔伯特·史密斯说。

text: Mapping means that farmers have to actually provide the geolocations of their plantation.
zh: 『mapping』(测绘定位)意味着农民必须真正提供其种植园的地理位置。

text: And you know, for some farmers that are very remote, that comes also with a challenge.
zh: 而你知道,对一些身处非常偏远地区的农民来说,这也带来了挑战。

text: Albert suggests that giving a geolocation is difficult for some farmers as they live in remote places and don't all have mobile phones.
zh: 阿尔伯特认为,提供地理位置对一些农民来说很困难,因为他们住在偏远地方,而且并非人人都有手机。

text: Geolocation is data taken from a special device which shows exactly where something is.
zh: 『geolocation』(地理定位)是从一种特殊设备获取的数据,能显示某物的确切位置。

text: And the term remote means at a large distance from places where other people live.
zh: 而『remote』这个词意思是距离其他人居住的地方很远。

text: Because of these changes, the price of chocolate might go up even more, meaning that chocolate lovers like us could really notice the difference in our bank accounts.
zh: 由于这些变化,巧克力的价格可能会涨得更高,这意味着像我们这样的巧克力爱好者,真的能在自己的银行账户上感受到差别。

text: Reporter Matthew Kenyon asked a chocolate lover on the street if they would pay more for their chocolate
zh: 记者马修·凯尼恩在街头问一位巧克力爱好者,他愿不愿意为自己的巧克力多付钱。

text: absolutely i would do that because i think the normal chocolates i are way too cheap definitely we will pay we have to pay more because the money will go to uh
zh: 当然,我会那么做,因为我觉得普通巧克力实在太便宜了;当然,我们会付、我们必须多付,因为这钱会给到,呃,

text: the local people who to give them a better life also to tackle deforestation
zh: 当地人,好让他们过上更好的生活,也用来应对森林砍伐。

text: and he asked another chocolate lover about whether he looks at where the cocoa beans come from for me personally absolutely not out of principle because
zh: 他还问了另一位巧克力爱好者,是否会留意可可豆的来源。对我个人来说,绝对不会,出于原则,因为,

text: i wanted to be produced as most efficiently as efficiently as possible.
zh: 我希望它被尽可能高效地生产出来。

text: The cost is completely irrelevant.
zh: 成本完全无关紧要。

text: OK.
zh: 好吧。

text: But on principle, I want production to be as efficient as possible.
zh: 但出于原则,我希望生产尽可能高效。

text: Otherwise it's a waste of everybody's time.
zh: 否则就是浪费大家的时间。

text: Mixed opinions it seems there, Becca.
zh: 看来意见不一啊,贝卡。

text: Whilst one chocolate lover would pay more for chocolate to support farmers and production, the other doesn't worry too much about the cost, but says the production process should be as efficient as possible.
zh: 一位巧克力爱好者愿意为支持农民和生产而多付钱,另一位则不太在意成本,却说生产过程应尽可能高效。

text: OK, time to answer our quiz question.
zh: 好,该回答我们的测验题了。

text: Earlier I asked you how many tonnes of cocoa were imported to Europe in 2023.
zh: 刚才我问你,2023年有多少吨可可进口到欧洲。

text: And you said 500 ,000 tonnes.
zh: 你说50万吨。

text: Well, Neil, I'll tell you, it is 2 million tonnes!
zh: 嗯,尼尔,我告诉你,是200万吨!

text: Wow.
zh: 哇。

text: Well, I was wrong and that really is a lot.
zh: 好吧,我错了,那真是好多啊。

text: Between us, we've probably eaten a few tonnes already this year.
zh: 咱俩加起来,今年大概已经吃掉好几吨了。

text: At least, Becca.
zh: 至少吧,贝卡。

text: I'd be interested to know what kind of chocolate our listeners enjoy.
zh: 我挺想知道我们的听众喜欢哪种巧克力。

text: Me too, and whether they would be happy to pay more for their favourite chocolate bar.
zh: 我也是,还有他们愿不愿意为自己最爱的那块巧克力多付钱。

text: OK, let's recap some of the vocabulary from this programme.
zh: 好,我们来回顾本期节目里的一些词汇。

text: Deforestation is the process of cutting down trees.
zh: 『deforestation』是砍伐树木的过程。

text: We heard co -op, which is a farm owned by its workers.
zh: 我们听到了『co-op』,指一个由其工人所有的农场。

text: A social enterprise is a business that wants to help the community.
zh: 『social enterprise』是一种想帮助社区的企业。

text: To trace something back to something is to find out where something's come from.
zh: 『trace something back to something』是查明某物来自哪里。

text: Geolocation is data taken from a device which can show you exactly where a person or thing is.
zh: 『geolocation』是从一种设备获取的数据,能向你显示一个人或物的确切位置。

text: And finally, remote means at a large distance from places where other people live.
zh: 最后,『remote』意思是距离其他人居住的地方很远。

text: Once again, our six minutes are up.
zh: 又一次,我们的六分钟到了。

text: But if you want to test your knowledge, visit our website bbclearningenglish .com, where you'll also find a worksheet and quiz for this episode.
zh: 但如果你想检验自己的知识,就来我们的网站bbclearningenglish.com,你还会在那儿找到本期的练习题和测验。

text: Bye.
zh: 拜拜。

text: See you next time.
zh: 下次见。

text: Goodbye.
zh: 再见。
